title: strophanthus wightianus
intro: Strophanthus wightianus, also known as the Indian strophanthus or Wight's strophanthus, is a plant species native to the Indian subcontinent and Southeast Asia. The plant has been traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine for its medicinal properties, including its cardiac glycoside content, which has been found to have a positive effect on the heart by slowing its rate and increasing its force of contraction. The health benefits of Strophanthus wightianus include its potential to treat conditions such as hypertension, cardiac arrhythmias, and congestive heart failure, as well as its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. Herbal preparations of Strophanthus wightianus are typically made from the plant's roots or leaves, and can be administered in various forms, including teas, tinctures, and capsules.
